data.job_description:
We are seeking a seasoned Finance Transformation Lead to drive the end-to-end modernization of our Order-to-Cash (O2C) and Procure-to-Pay (P2P) towers. This is a high-impact, strategic role requiring a leader capable of managing complex, concurrent initiatives across technology deployment and process harmonization. The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between legacy operations and future-state automation, leveraging deep domain expertise to standardize global processes while overseeing the deployment of cutting-edge platforms such as HighRadius and ReadSoft. This role demands exceptional governance capabilities, rigorous documentation standards, and the ability to influence C-level stakeholders in a matrixed environment.

Key responsibility:
• Strategic Roadmap Execution: Define and execute the global transformation roadmap for O2C and P2P, ensuring alignment with broader corporate finance strategies and achieving targeted efficiency gains (cost, speed, quality).
• Dual-Track Project Leadership: Lead multiple parallel initiatives, balancing technology implementations (e.g., SAP S/4HANA migration, HighRadius deployment) with non-technology workstreams such as global process harmonization, policy standardization, and master data governance.
• Technology Integration & Automation: Drive the adoption of HighRadius for AR automation (Cash Application, Collections, Deductions) and ReadSoft for AP invoice automation, ensuring seamless integration with the core SAP landscape.
• Process Excellence & Harmonization: Champion the standardization of O2C and P2P processes across diverse geographies to eliminate

redundancy, reducing manual touchpoints and enforcing Global Process Owner (GPO) standards.
• Governance & Compliance: Establish and maintain a rigorous project governance framework; oversee schedule adherence, budget management, and risk mitigation while ensuring strict compliance with SOX and internal audit requirements.
• Stakeholder Management: Act as the primary interface between Finance, IT, and Business Operations; conduct high-level steering committee presentations and manage expectations of CFOs, Controllers, and regional finance heads.
• Vendor & SLA Management: oversee external vendor performance for outsourced components and technology partners, ensuring strict adherence to SLAs and KPIs.
• Documentation & Knowledge Management: Enforce superior documentation standards for process maps (L1-L5), SOPs, and technical requirement documents; leverage ServiceNow for knowledge base management.

Qualification:
• Education: Master’s degree in finance, Accounting, or Business Administration; CPA/ACCA/CIMA or MBA preferred.
• Experience: Minimum 12-15 years of progressive experience in Finance Transformation, Shared Services, or Consulting (Big 4 background preferred).
• Domain Expertise: SME-level knowledge of O2C (Credit, Collections, Cash App) and P2P (Vendor Master, PO/Non-PO Invoice processing, Payments).
• Project Management: Proven track record of managing large-scale, multi-geography transformation projects exceeding $5M+ budget.

Technical skill
• Core Platforms: Expert-level knowledge of SAP FI/CO (S/4HANA experience will be advantage).
• Specialized O2C Automation: Hands-on experience with HighRadiusor similar advanced AR automation suites.
• Specialized P2P Automation: Deep understanding of ReadSoft / Kofax for invoice processing and OCR technologies.
• Workflow & Governance: Proficiency in ServiceNow for case management and knowledge management.
• General: Advanced proficiency in MS Office Suite, MS Project, and Visio.

Core Competencies
• Stakeholder Management: Ability to navigate complex political landscapes and influence without direct authority.
• Communication: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; adept at converting technical issues into business impacts for executive audiences.
• Presentation: Mastery in creatingexecutive-level decks and presenting to Steering Committees.
• Documentation: Meticulous attention to detail in project artifacts, process flows, and governance reporting.

At NiCE, we don’t limit our challenges. We challenge our limits. Always. We’re ambitious. We’re game changers. And we play to win. We set the highest standards and execute beyond them. And if you’re like us, we can offer you the ultimate career opportunity that will light a fire within you.

So, what’s the role all about?

The Senior Financial Analyst is responsible for providing business performance analysis, reporting, and forecasting. Also responsible for providing a variety of financial data and maintaining and updating financial models and plans and provide ad hoc analysis for the organization, budgeting, long-term strategic planning and resource allocation processes.

How will you make an impact?
• Financial planning and budgeting – you will be primarily responsible for creating and updating financial forecasts. Additionally, you will be a point of contact for financial modeling, budgeting, and business planning.
• Reporting and analysis – help create meaningful ad-hoc and standard reports and refine existing ones. Each month, you will also analyze actuals and compare to the forecast/budgets. Analyzes and communicates weekly metrics, monthly results, and variance analysis, quarterly and annual projections.
• Special Projects – you will need to be agile as new projects, initiatives, and opportunities present themselves. Projects give you the opportunity to grow and become involved in things that go beyond your traditional analyst roles.
• Modeling – you will build models (some sophisticated, some simple) from scratch and improve existing models. Creativity and new ideas are welcome here.
• Data analysis – the business will look to you to help understand, analyze, and draw conclusions from data to assist in decision making.

Have you got what it takes?
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, Economics, Business or any related discipline preferred
• Minimum 5 years of relevant FP&A experience
• Advanced knowledge of Excel
• Demonstrated knowledge of financial analysis and modeling
• Ability to work under pressure on multiple projects and deadlines
• Ability to effectively prioritize workload and manage changes in direction of the company
• Strong interpersonal skills, with an ability to work cooperatively and collaboratively with all levels of employees and management to maximize performance
• Ability to act and operate independently with minimal daily direction
• Confidence to liaise and present to Senior Management
• Desire to work in a matrixed environment
• Accuracy and attention to details

Bonus Experience:
• Intermediate skill level in MS Office applications
• Experience with Power BI and Oracle
• Prior Technology industry experience

What’s in it for you?

Join an ever-growing, market disrupting, global company where the teams – comprised of the best of the best – work in a fast-paced, collaborative, and creative environment! As the market leader, every day at NiCE is a chance to learn and grow, and there are endless internal career opportunities across multiple roles, disciplines, domains, and locations. If you are passionate, innovative, and excited to constantly raise the bar, you may just be our next NiCEr!

Enjoy NiCE-FLEX!

At NiCE, we work according to the NiCE-FLEX hybrid model, which enables maximum flexibility: 2 days working from the office and 3 days of remote work, each week. Naturally, office days focus on face-to-face meetings, where teamwork and collaborative thinking generate innovation, new ideas, and a vibrant, interactive atmosphere.

Requisition ID: 10275

Reporting into: Group Manager

Role Type: Individual Contributor

About NiCE

NICE Ltd. (NASDAQ: NICE) software products are used by 25,000+ global businesses, including 85 of the Fortune 100 corporations, to deliver extraordinary customer experiences, fight financial crime and ensure public safety. Every day, NiCE software manages more than 120 million customer interactions and monitors 3+ billion financial transactions.

Known as an innovation powerhouse that excels in AI, cloud and digital, NiCE is consistently recognized as the market leader in its domains, with over 8,500 employees across 30+ countries.

NiCE is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, neurotype, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation or any other category protected by law.

Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A)

Who we are?

Johnson Controls is the global leader for smart, healthy and sustainable buildings. At Johnson Controls, we’ve been making buildings smarter since 1885, and our capabilities, depth of innovation experience, and global reach have been growing ever since. Today, we offer the world’s largest portfolio of building products, technologies, software, and services; we put that portfolio to work to transform the environments where people live, work, learn and play. This is where Johnson Controls comes in, helping drive the outcomes that matter most. Through a full range of systems and digital solutions, we make your buildings smarter. A smarter building is safer, more comfortable, more efficient, and, ultimately, more sustainable. Most important, smarter buildings let you focus more intensely on your unique mission. Better for your people. Better for your bottom line. Better for the planet. We’re helping to create a healthy planet with solutions that decrease energy use, reduce waste and make carbon neutrality a reality. Sustainability is a top priority for our company. We committed to invest 75 percent of new product development R&D in climate-related innovation to develop sustainable products and services. We take sustainability seriously. Achieving net zero carbon emissions before 2040 is just one of our commitments to making the world a better place. Please visit and follow Johnson Controls LinkedIn for recent exciting activities.

Why JCI: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nrbigjbpxkg Asia-Pacific LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/johnson-controls-asia pacific/posts/?feedView=all Career: The Power Behind Your Mission OpenBlue: This is How a Space Comes Alive

How will you do it?

The senior finance analysis is responsible for handling FP&A matters, including coordination with the functional department and business operations. He or She has the overall responsibility for preparing the HQ level reporting on a monthly basis and outlook rolling report by quarter and consolidated all HQ financial statements. He or she also has the responsibility of monitoring of the overall compliance with Johnson Controls´ finance policies, procedures and timelines applicable to the respective country’s legal entities.

Primary Duties:

Business partner

1.Review all of cost center and make appropriate accrual to ensure the P&L is fluctuated on a reasonable or explainable level.

2. MEC activities and variance to normal.

3. Monitor each cost center and make sure the allocation to be done.

Financial Reporting:

1. Assist the uploading work to HFM Essbase for yearly planning and rolling forecast on a quarterly basis.

2. Prepare and report to the HQ’ flash and outlook per timeline.

3. Provide the details actual data vs plan vs last year report to different functions. Finance function:

1. Work closely with functional department for all end-to-end finance support, big projects’ expense and progress tracking to month-end closing.

2. Track to actual data with trend flux analyze rolling 3 months.

Skills and Competencies Required:

· Bachelor’s degree with accounting/finance major

· Previous financial and management reporting experience with an Multinational Corporate Finance or American company

· 5+ Years of relevant work experience

· Good command of English

· In depth knowledge of U.S. GAAP

· Good SAP knowledge with hands-on experience using the ERP

· Good Excel and Hyperion Financial Management system

· Excellent interpersonal

· Strong customer service focus

What we offer:

We offer an exciting and challenging position. Joining us you will become part of a leading global multi industrial corporation defined by its stimulating work environment and job satisfaction. In addition, we offer outstanding career development opportunities which will stretch your abilities and channel your talents

Diversity & Inclusion Our dedication to diversity and inclusion starts with our values. We lead with integrity and purpose, focusing on the future and aligning with our customers’ vision for success. Our High Performance Culture ensures that we have the best talent that is highly engaged and eager to innovate.  Our D&I mission elevates each employee’s responsibility to contribute to our culture. It’s through these contributions that we’ll drive the mindsets and behaviors we need to power our customers’ missions. You have the power. You have the voice. You have the culture in your hands.

data.employer_name: S. S. CHAVAN & ASSOCIATES CHARTERED ACCOUNTANTS
data.job_description:
Hiring: Paid Assistant / Semi-Qualified CA (CA Inter) – Pune

We are a growing Partnership Firm in Kothrud, Pune, specializing in Accounting, Taxation, and Auditing. We are looking for a dedicated Semi-Qualified CA (CA Inter) to join our team and handle diverse assignments.

Key Responsibilities:
• End-to-end Accounting and Finalization of Accounts.
• Statutory & Internal Audits (including Trust Audits and Tax Audits).
• Compliance management for GST and Income Tax.
• International Taxation (15CA/CB & Equalisation Levy)
• Virtual CFO Services (MIS, Budgeting & Cash Flow)
• Labor Law & ROC Compliances

Candidate Profile:
• Appaeated for CA Inter or CA Final .
• Prior experience in Finalization and Audit at traditonal CA practice Firm is preferred .
• Strong understanding of Tax compliance.

Work-Life & Growth:
• Location: Paud Road, Kothrud, Pune.
• CA Final Leaves: We support your professional growth;
study leaves will be granted based on mutual understanding.

This role will sit within the FP&A Analytics & Digitization team, within the Finance FP&A organization and will be responsible for developing and delivering Financial Data assets aligned with Core ERP systems in service of business needs.Will work to support a C&P, Midstream and Oil & Gas business.Customer & products• Gas & low carbon energy• Production & operations• Innovation & engineering• Regions, cities & solutions• Strategy & sustainability• Trading & Shipping• Communications & advocacy• Finance• Legal• People & cultureAbout the roleThe FP&A Analytics & Digitization Advisor/Manager develops and delivers Financial Data assets aligned with Core ERP systems, supplying to the Mobility, Convenience & Midstream business. It also supports the Global Data Office business collaborators, ensuring regional activities meet global metrics, benchmarks, and reporting standards. The incumbent would be potentially required to work under alternative shift arrangements to ensure appropriate overlap hours with Eastern Standard time or Asia Pacific time zones.Key Roles & Responsibilities• Engagement with a business solution delivery approach: Provide business and technical support, including defect resolution and process training, or bring up issues as needed. Collaborate with the FP&A team to resolve conflicts and understand team duties relative to other interested parties.• Practitioner of Agile methods to deliver solutions: Practice agile methodology to problem identification, project management and solutioning towards self service models.• Uphold collaborator service levels: Implement support tasks according to instructional materials and Service Level Agreements, addressing general support questions from FP&A Product Team and Business teams.• Collaboration: Work with the FP&A Analytics and Digitization Manager to prioritize Product support activities. Engage with FP&A teams to address concerns or questions, providing business and technical support.• Continuous improvement: Identify and implement opportunities for process improvement based on experience and findings.• Data management: support broader safe data culture and implement data governance framework in the business in line with global standards.• Leadership: Lead the MI team in developing new solutions, focusing on datasets, data flows, and visualization. Manage work prioritization within the team. Build capability within the team in data asset delivery and collaborator engagement. Identify and build a future talent pipeline through a clear succession and capability plan.Must have educational qualifications:Business/Finance or Engineering Field Degree level or equivalent or in Data Analytics or related field.Preferred education/certifications:Masters/Bachelor's in computer science/relatedMinimum years of confirmed experience:10 years of relevant post degree experience, working across multiple segmentsPreferred experience:Experience within global, complex and matrix organizations, preferably within an Oil & Gas business or experience in industries such as Retail, Asset-based business, Manufacturing, or Logistics.Must have experiences/skills (To be hired with):• Proficiency in SAP/ERP systems, SQL, Power BI, and data visualization tools.• Experience with Business MI, report building, Data Ownership, Architecture, and Governance processes• Knowledge and experience with Data Visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau)• Hands-on experience in AWS• Skills in various project delivery methodologies, including Agile methodologies' practitioner.• Leadership in driving team performance, forming partnerships, and driving transformation and excellenceYou will be working with a team of finance professionals as part of the Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) organization. The FP&A organization is an exciting new team being established by bp to create a center of expertise in the areas of business planning, budgeting and forecasting, financial analysis and economic evaluation. The role will regularly interact and be the main contact point for Business/Functions seniorleadership.In addition to the FP&A team, you will also be partnering with the local finance team and various technical and leadership teams in onsite locations.About bpOur purpose is to deliver energy to the world, today and tomorrow. For over 100 years, bp has focused on discovering, developing, and producing oil and gas in the nations where we operate. We are one of the few companies globally that can provide governments and customers with an integrated energy offering. Delivering our strategy sustainably is fundamental to achieving our ambition to be a net zero company by 2050 or sooner!We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.Additional InformationWe are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.Even though the job is advertised as full time, please contact the hiring manager or the recruiter as flexible working arrangements may be considered.

**Job Description: Senior Analyst, Strategic Finance**
• *Role Overview:**
Evolent Health is seeking a Senior Analyst for the Strategic Finance team to support the Product and Technology Business Line. In this role, you will be responsible for supporting the company's financial planning, forecasting, budgeting, and reporting functions. You will collaborate closely with Accounting, Procurement, and department heads to provide insights and financial analysis that drive business decisions. The ideal candidate will have experience in managing monthly accruals, accounting processes, procurement processes, and possess strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to enhance business performance.
• *Key Responsibilities:**
- Work closely with the accounting team to ensure proper documentation and recording of accruals, journal entries, and other financial transactions
- Provide financial oversight of procurement activities and ensure proper accounting treatment of purchasing and vendor transactions
- Maintain and update existing standard models and contribute to the development of new initiatives
- Manage the monthly accrual process to ensure accurate recognition of expenses in accordance with accounting principles and company policies
- Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting
- Support monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting and budgeting cycles with KPIs and insights tailored to Technology and Product leadership
- Maintain, create, automate, and improve Adaptive Insights financial models and reporting templates
- Liaise with other teams under the Finance umbrella to maintain accurate forecasting and partner on month-end variance analyses
- Support special projects, including ROI analysis for new Technology investments, Product launches, and platform enhancements
• *Qualifications Required:**
- Chartered Accountant/CPA, CMA, or Masters degree in Business Finance or equivalent experience
- 3-5 years of experience as an Analyst or Associate in corporate finance (FP&A;), investment banking, or other Finance role with financial modeling experience
- Proactive problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently
- Experience working with Accounts Payable, Accounting, and Procurement teams for invoice, Purchase Order, and accrual related items
- Extensive experience using Microsoft Excel
- Strong analytical abilities with a track record of identifying insights from financial information and translating into business analytics and recommendations with a high level of accuracy

Evolent Health is seeking a Senior Analyst for the Strategic Finance team to support the Product and Technology Business Line. In this role, you will be responsible for supporting the company's financial planning, forecasting, budgeting, and reporting functions. You will collaborate closely with Accounting, Procurement, and department heads to provide insights and financial analysis that drive business decisions. The ideal candidate will have experience in managing monthly accruals, accounting processes, procurement processes, and possess strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to enhance business performance.
• *Key Responsibilities:**
- Work closely with the accounting team to ensure proper documentation and recording of accruals, journal entries, and other financial transactions
- Provide financial oversight of procurement activities and ensure proper accounting treatment of purchasing and vendor transactions

Job Description: Assistant Manager/Manager - Accounting/Finance Manager

Location: Tribe, Viman Nagar, Pune

Type: Full-Time

Experience: 5+ years (Relevant)

Qualification: CA

Software Knowledge: Tally (Mandatory), MS Excel & Word

About Tribe:

Tribe is more than just a student accommodation facility;
we are a vibrant community that fosters creativity, collaboration, and comfort. Our mission is to redefine the living experience for students and young professionals, and we take pride in creating extraordinary experiences for our residents.

Role Overview:

We are looking for an experienced and detail-oriented Assistant Manager/Manager – Accounting to join our growing team at Tribe. In this role, you will be responsible for overseeing core accounting functions, managing financial data with precision, and ensuring timely statutory compliance.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Accounting Operations:

- Responsible for Sales and Purchase Accounting.

- Maintain accurate records of debtors and creditors.

- Responsible for bank reconciliations and oversee online/offline payments.

2. Compliance & Reporting:

- Prepare and file TDS, EPF, ESIC, and GST returns within statutory timelines.

- Coordinate with external auditors for monthly and annual compliances.

3. Documentation & Record-Keeping:

- Manage accounting documentation, filing, and record maintenance.

- Process invoices, expense forms, and payment requests.

4. MIS & Analysis:

- Generate various MIS reports for internal use and decision-making.

- Assist in financial planning and analysis as required.

What We’re Looking For:

- Strong understanding of accounting principles and financial regulations.

- Highest level of proficiency in Direct and Indirect Tax compliances.

- Proficient in Tally (v9) and MS Excel & Word.

- Highly organized and detail-oriented with the ability to multitask.

- Strong communication skills and a proactive approach.

- Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.

Preferred Skills:

- Experience in managing accounting processes for startups or hospitality/residential brands.

- Familiarity with online banking platforms and digital payment tools.

- Exposure to handling audits and working closely with CA firms.

What You’ll Gain:

- The opportunity to work with a premium student accommodation brand.

- A dynamic and inclusive work environment.

- Hands-on experience with end-to-end financial operations.

- A platform to grow professionally and make a tangible impact.

To Apply:

Send your resume and a short cover letter to with the subject line "Application for Assistant Manager/Manager - Accounting".

Join Tribe. Build systems. Drive efficiency. Grow with us.

data.employer_name: Milind Sangoram and Company, Chartered Accountants
data.job_description:
Milind Sangoram and Company, Chartered Accountants is a dynamic and innovative firm committed to providing exceptional financial services to our clients. We specialize in corporate taxation and audit, and our team is dedicated to delivering tailored solutions to meet our clients' diverse needs. As part of our commitment to nurturing talent, we are excited to announce opportunities in the area of Tax and Audit..

Role Description

This is a full-time on-site Audit and Tax Manager role located in Pune. The person will be responsible for assisting the Chartered Accountants in preparing financial statements, bookkeeping, auditing, and taxation. The candidate will also have to collate and analyze data from various sources, create reports, and assist in formulating business strategies.

Qualifications
• Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field with 3 years of experience in Accounting or has completed their CA articleship.
• Good working knowledge of accounting principles and standards
• Excellent numerical and analytical skills
• Strong communication and interpersonal skills
• Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
• Proficient in MS Office and accounting software
• Interest in the field of finance and taxation

data.job_title: Senior Consultant | Onestream: All | Pune | Finance Transformation
data.job_description:
Your potential, unleashed.

India’s impact on the global economy has increased at an exponential rate and Deloitte presents an opportunity to unleash and realise your potential amongst cutting edge leaders, and organisations shaping the future of the region, and indeed, the world beyond.

At Deloitte, your whole self to work, every day. Combine that with our drive to propel with purpose and you have the perfect playground to collaborate, innovate, grow, and make an impact that matters.

The team

Enterprise technology has to do much more than keep the wheels turning; it is the engine that drives functional excellence and the enabler of innovation and long-term growth.Learn more about ET&P

Your work profile

As a Senior Consultant in our Emerging ET&P Team you’ll build and nurture positive working relationships with teams and clients with the intention to exceed client expectations: -
• Should have worked on or have in-depth knowledge on Financial Planning and Analysis activities
• Should be able to design, build, configure and deploy applications based on CPM business requirements and processes
• Should be able to understand the client requirements and convert the same into technical solutions
• Should have experience working with Onestream solutions and implementations
• End-to-end experience required in Onestream developments - Building dimensions, metadata, Dashboards, Workflows, CubeViews, Reports
• Should possess applying knowledge of VB.Net in building and implementing Financial solutions using Onestream
• Required Good communication and listening skills with positive attitude towards learning and challenges
• Should have data analysis and presentation skills

Education Qualifications – 3 to 5 years of experience with VB.Net and SQL and a minimum of 2+ year of experience implementing ERP, EPM and Financial Close and Consolidation solutions

Job Overview
• Should be able to design, build, configure and deploy applications based on CPM business requirements and processes
• Should be able to understand the client requirements, redesign business processes, applications and system designs
• Hands on experience with OneStream XF, SAP BPC, Hyperion Financial Management, Hyperion Financial Data Quality Management solutions and implementations
• Should have good understanding of ERP, EPM systems and processes including Account Reconciliation, GL, Financial Planning and Budgeting, Consolidation
• Should have ability to evaluate out of box functionalities and utilize them to build enhanced solutions

Technology & Transformation - ET&P: OneStream
• Required excellent communication and presentation skills to communicate ideas and solutions to customers, team members and partners
• Should possess an ability to lead and work within and outside team to ensure timely delivery of project deliverables

Skills Required
• Qualifications:
• Finance Background recommended, MBA / PG / CA/ CFA
• Overall 4 to 6 years of experience with ERP and EPM solutions and a minimum of 2+ year of experience working in OneStream
• Experience Between 6 – 8 years of relevant experience across consulting and/or industry roles, post qualification would be preferred
• Prior experience managing end to end client engagements and programs
• Prior experience in managing teams
• * Skills and Competencies Ability to challenge the status quo, deliver compelling and well-structured arguments for change and transformation
• Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
• Willingness and ability to take initiative and learn independently
• Proficient in Microsoft Office suite of products such as PowerPoint, Visio, Excel, Word etc.
• Willingness to travel - This role required travel depending on project requirements
• Develop high-performing people and teams through challenging and meaningful opportunities
• Deliver exceptional client service; maximize results and drive high performance from people while fostering collaboration across businesses and borders.
• Influence clients, teams, and individuals positively, leading by example and establishing confident relationships with increasingly senior people
• Understand key objectives for clients and Deloitte; align people to objectives and set priorities and direction.
• Acts as a role model, embracing and living our purpose and values, and recognizing others for the impact they make
• Your role as a leader

At Deloitte India, we believe in the importance of leadership at all levels. We expect our people to embrace and live our purpose by challenging themselves to identify issues that are most important for our clients, our people, and for society and make an impact that matters.

In addition to living our purpose, your role across our organization helps us :

Location and way of working
• Location: Bangalore, Mumbai, Gurugram and Noida
• This profile involves occasional travelling to client locations OR this profile does not involve extensive travel for work.
• Hybrid is our default way of working. Each domain has customised the hybrid approach to their unique needs.

Technology & Transformation - ET&P: OneStream

How you’ll grow

Connect for impact

Our exceptional team of professionals across the globe are solving some of the world’s most complex business problems, as well as directly supporting our communities, the planet, and each other. Know more in our Global Impact Report and our India Impact Report.

Empower to lead

You can be a leader irrespective of your career level. Our colleagues are characterised by their ability to inspire, support, and provide opportunities for people to deliver their best and grow both as professionals and human beings. Know more about Deloitte and our One Young World partnership.

Inclusion for all

At Deloitte, people are valued and respected for who they are and are trusted to add value to their clients, teams and communities in a way that reflects their own unique capabilities. Know more about everyday steps that you can take to be more inclusive. At Deloitte, we believe in the unique skills, attitude and potential each and every one of us brings to the table to make an impact that matters.

Drive your career

At Deloitte, you are encouraged to take ownership of your career. We recognise there is no one size fits all career path, and global, cross-business mobility and up / re-skilling are all within the range of possibilities to shape a unique and fulfilling career. Know more about Life at Deloitte.

Everyone’s welcome… entrust your happiness to us

Our workspaces and initiatives are geared towards your 360-degree happiness. This includes specific needs you may have in terms of accessibility, flexibility, safety and security, and caregiving. Here’s a glimpse of things that are in store for you.

Interview tips

We want job seekers exploring opportunities at Deloitte to feel prepared, confident and comfortable. To help you with your interview, we suggest that you do your research, know some background about the organisation and the business area you’re applying to. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte profession

data.employer_name: M D O & Company, Chartered Accountants
data.job_description:
M D O & Company, Chartered Accountants, is looking to hire CA Article Assistants .

Qualifications: CA intermediate both group cleared

Job Description:
• Virtual CFO Services
• Accounting
• Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable Management
• Preparation of financial statements
• Statutory Audit
• Internal Audit
• Business Valuation
• Tax Compliance: GST and TDS
• Preparation of MIS

Experience: CA intermediate both groups cleared

Location: Office no.22, Vastushree complex, near Hyde Park, Bibwewadi, Pune-411037

Role : Partner Chartered Accountant

Qualification : CA

Location : Nariman Point

Experience : 10 to 12 years

Job Description :

The Partner – Direct Tax (Financial Services) will lead and grow the Firm’s Direct Tax practice for Financial Services clients, including Private Equity, Venture Capital, Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), Asset Managers, Banks, NBFCs, and Insurance companies. The role involves strategic client relationship management, complex tax advisory, deal structuring, litigation oversight, and business development.

Key Responsibilities

Client Relationship & Practice Leadership
• Lead key Financial Services client relationships and act as a trusted tax advisor to senior stakeholders.
• Drive practice growth through client origination, cross-functional collaboration, and market-facing initiatives.
• Support pitches, proposals, and development of new FS tax opportunities across Markets and Deals teams.

Fund Structuring & Financial Services Advisory
• Advise on structuring of AIFs from conceptualization to SEBI approval.
• Provide end-to-end tax advisory for domestic and offshore fund structures, including GIFT IFSC.
• Review and advise on fund documentation including PPMs, SHAs, tax chapters, and investor communications.
• Advise on waterfall mechanisms, distribution models, and carried interest computations.

Deals & Transaction Tax
• Advise PE/VC/Real Estate funds on transaction structures, capital instruments, liquidation preferences, ratchets, slump sales, LBOs, and offshore restructurings.
• Lead tax due diligence (buy-side and sell-side) and provide transaction execution support including tax opinions.
• Review SPAs, SHAs, indemnities, representations & warranties, and tax insurance policies.

Investment Lifecycle & Exit Advisory
• Advise on tax-efficient exit strategies including secondary sales and IPOs.
• Support pre-IPO restructuring, especially treatment of convertible instruments.
• Review financial models and valuation reports from tax and regulatory perspectives.

Compliance & Reporting
• Oversee direct tax compliances including returns, tax audits, FATCA-CRS, and regulatory filings with SEBI/RBI.
• Review financial statements under IGAAP and IFRS for funds, managers, and portfolio companies.

Litigation & Regulatory Matters
• Independently manage tax litigation up to CIT (Appeals) level.
• Coordinate and support counsels on matters before ITAT, High Courts, and Supreme Court.
• Advise on regulatory matters involving SEBI, RBI, and other authorities.

Qualifications & Experience
• Chartered Accountant.
• 10+ years of post-qualification experience in Direct Tax with strong Financial Services exposure.
• Prior experience in Big 4 or leading professional services firms.
• Proven expertise in fund taxation, transaction tax, litigation, and regulatory advisory.

Key Skills
• Deep technical knowledge of Indian Direct Tax laws
• Strong commercial and transaction structuring acumen
• Client leadership and stakeholder management
• Team leadership, mentoring, and business development.

Job Details

About the job

Our team members are at the heart of everything we do. At Cencora, we are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures, and every person here is essential to us being able to deliver on that purpose. If you want to make a difference at the center of health, come join our innovative company and help us improve the lives of people and animals everywhere. Apply today!

Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Finance & Accounting professional to manage end-to-end accounting, financial reporting, statutory compliance, and internal controls. The role is critical to ensuring accurate financial statements, regulatory compliance, effective cash flow management, and timely statutory and vendor payments.

Key Responsibilities

• Own monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements and close process

• Lead statutory, tax, and internal audits; ensure a strong control environment and full compliance with SOX and Internal controls over financial reporting.

• Manage direct and indirect tax compliance (TDS, GST, advance tax, corporate tax), working closely with internal team as well as advisors

• Oversee payroll accounting and statutory compliance (PF, ESI, NPS)

• Ensure strong balance-sheet controls, reconciliations, and cash flow management

• Manage fixed asset accounting, capitalization, and physical verification

• Oversee accounts payable, vendor reconciliations, statutory/vendor payments and travel & reimbursements

• Ensure compliance with RBI, MCA, FEMA, labor laws and other regulatory compliances and proactively engage with relevant stakeholders on upcoming changes

• Drive process improvements, controls, and reporting quality
• Serve as the primary Accounting and Finance point of contact (POC) for senior leadership, collaborating closely with Directors and HR Partners to provide oversight, guidance, and strategic support for departmental initiatives.
• Supports Intercompany(IC) billing and collection and ensuring elimination with IC by maintaining appropriate reconciliations.

Qualifications & Experience
• Chartered Accountant (CA) certification required.
• 12–15 years of experience in financial accounting and controllership with with at least 3+ years in a senior controllership or finance leadership role.
• Strong knowledge of Indian tax, regulatory, and compliance requirements
• Experience with ERP systems and internal controls
• Proven experience managing shared services or global capability centers supporting multinational operations is strongly preferred
• Public accounting experience at a Big 4 firm is strongly preferred
• Stakeholder Influence: Necessary for engaging with local/global leadership, local auditors/advisor, and regulatory bodies.
• Excellent verbal and written communication skills; ability to present findings to executive leadership.
• This role has potential to grow to a regional controller role of South-Asia, so multi region experience is an advantage.

Schedule

Full time

Affiliated Companies
Affiliated Companies: CENCORA BUSINESS SERVICES INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED

Equal Employment Opportunity

Cencora is committed to providing equal employment opportunity without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or membership in any other class protected by federal, state or local law.

The company’s continued success depends on the full and effective utilization of qualified individuals. Therefore, harassment is prohibited and all matters related to recruiting, training, compensation, benefits, promotions and transfers comply with equal opportunity principles and are non-discriminatory.

Cencora is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities during the employment process which are consistent with legal requirements. If you wish to request an accommodation while seeking employment, please call 888.692.2272 or email hrsc@cencora.com. We will make accommodation determinations on a request-by-request basis. Messages and emails regarding anything other than accommodations requests will not be returned

Senior FP&A Analyst

Come work at a place where innovation and teamwork come together to support the most exciting missions in the world!

Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst (FP&A)

Job Description:

Qualys is looking for a Senior Financial Analyst with strong Excel and financial modeling skills to support cost management across the business. This role partners closely with FP&A and business teams on budgeting, forecasting, and long‑range planning, leveraging regular forecast updates to deliver accurate forecasts and actionable insights to the Manager/Team lead.

What you’ll do:
• Support with annual budget and rolling forecasts
• Manage headcount and vendor cost forecasting by handling day-to-day activities related to forecasting/planning as required on a regular basis
• Support month‑end close (accruals, reclasses, HC role forward, allocations, additional tasks, as required)
• Assist/Build forecast models, review decks, and analytics tools
• Partner cross‑functionally, including with US (PST) stakeholders as required.
• Perform ad hoc financial analysis, tool/report testing, and drive process improvements - short-term and long-term.
• Over time, the role will require the ability to take ownership and be responsible for complex analyses/projects, as required.

What we’re looking for:
• Master’s in Finance/Accounting (or related)
• 3–6 years of FP\&A, accounting, or analytical experience
• Advanced Excel skills; modeling experience required
• Strong understanding of financial statements and cost drivers
• Experience with NetSuite, Coupa, Anaplan, and Power Query is a plus
• Detail‑oriented, proactive, strong communication, and an observant team player
• Interested in learning AI and new analytical tools, and assisting in the development and testing of new automation projects

Location: Pune (Hybrid – 3 days office/2 days WFH)
Flexibility: During quarter end, month end and overlap with PST, as required

Experience Level
Senior Level

Ashok Chhajed & Associates is 40 years old Firm having Head Office at Ahmedabad and branches on PAN India basis which is having Assignments of all kind of industries.

The Firm is recruiting a Chartered Accountant having Post qualification experience of 7 to 10 years in the field of Account, Audit, Income Tax and GST who will be deployed in PSU at Pune on behalf of us.

The applicant not having knowledge of INDAs and SAP should not apply the same

Pay: ₹125,000.00 - ₹175,000.00 per month

• Audit and Accounts along with Taxation: 7 years (Preferred)

• Chartered Accountant (Preferred)

data.job_title: Fund Services.PE Senior Fund Accountant | Pune, IN
data.job_description:
Fund Services.PE Senior Fund Accountant

Job Description

Our Benefits

Your wellbeing is of paramount importance to us, and central to our success. We provide a range of benefits, training and education support, and flexible working arrangements to help you achieve success in your career while balancing personal needs. Ask us about specific benefits in your location.

We embrace diversity, prioritizing the hiring of people from diverse backgrounds. Our inclusive culture is a source of pride and strength, fostering innovation and mutual respect.

Citco welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection.

Responsibilities

Your Role:

• Maintaining books and records and calculating fund Net Asset Values;
• Reviewing and/or preparing financial statements and associated reports, including performance metrics;
• Reviewing and/or calculating carried interest/performance/incentive fees;
• Reviewing and/or preparing calculations and investor allocations for capital calls, distributions and equalisations;
• Coordinating and/or processing payments and other transactions;
• Acting as a primary day-to-day contact with clients and other fund participants;
• Work closely with the Investor Relations team to respond to investor queries and to disseminate capital activity and other investor notices;
• Participating in projects that may be defined from time to time in connection with the continuing expansion and evolution of the business;
• Identifying and escalating any material issues effecting service delivery;
• Any other functions required as part of the business of the Company.
Qualifications

About You:

• Bachelor's degree in Accounting
• Professional accounting qualification - CA, CPA, ACCA or similar
• Minimum of 2 - 4 years' experience in accounting for the financial services sector, particularly Private Equity, Asset Management or Fund Administration.
• Working knowledge of Investran highly desirable (or other Private Equity system e.g. E-Front)
• Experience in Limited Partnership accounting
• Private equity/real estate fund administration experience
• Experienced in reviewing and preparing general ledger and financial statements.
• Proficiency in English (both verbal and written).
• Motivated, bright and diligent individual who is driven to meet deadlines.
• A team player who is able to manage individual responsibilities.
• Sound judgment, problem solving and analytical skills.
• Proficient with Microsoft Applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint etc.).

data.employer_name: Emerson Electric Company India Pvt Ltd
data.job_description:
FP&A Lead - PRM BU MEA

PRM BU Lead – FP&A

Job Summary

If you are a Finance professional, Emerson has an exciting role for you! We are looking for a PRM Business Unit Lead to work with our FP&A COE in Pune for MEA world area.

We value autonomy, self-reliance, fast movers, a passion for robust and ability to maintained quality of financial reporting.

In This Role, Your Responsibilities Will Be:
• Manage Financial reporting process, ensure timely and accurate reporting of financial information to internal and external stakeholders.
• Responsible for monthly, quarterly, and annual financial closes, meeting reporting deadlines and explaining variations.
• Support on Annual Budgets, mid-year reviews and forecast processes.
• Assist in the preparation of financial presentations such as Management pack, POR slides to BU Leadership teams.
• Support on Orders POR process, analyze actual vs POR, identify drivers & commentaries
• Drive inventory analysis and ownership of Trade working capital (TWC)
• Support on CAPEX actual vs POR analysis and commentaries
• Drive Process improvement and Automation Projects to create efficiency
• Statutory audit support and ensure compliance with internal controls
• Collaborate with cross functional teams and work as a Finance business partner
• Drive annual performance and team development planning
• Support on interviews and onboarding of new members
• Continuous development and research of accounting principles and practices including IFRS.
• Support in Ad-hoc requests

Who You Are:

You promote high visibility of shared contributions to goals. You quickly and significantly take action in constantly evolving, unexpected situations. You actively seek input from pertinent sources to make timely and well-informed decisions. You handle the risk that comes with moving forward when the outcome is not certain.

For This Role, You Will Need:
• Finance Professional with 7+ years of work experience in FP&A Domain
• Hands on experience in ERP, MS Office, and reporting tools
• Creative and Analytical thinking ability and Leadership skills
• Self-starter, suggesting and implementing improvements to the processes
• Balance stakeholder, Situational adaptability, manages complexities & Manages ambiguity.
• Qualified Chartered Accountant or MBA in Finance
• Basic understanding of Accounting Principles and US GAAP
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills
• System Knowledge of Oracle, Hyperion Financial Management
• Knowledge of Project Cost Accounting will be an advantage
• Knowledge of Power BI or related presentation tools

Preferred Qualifications that Set You Apart:

Our Offer to You:

By joining Emerson, you will be given the opportunity to make a difference through the work you do.

Emerson's compensation and benefits programs are designed to be competitive within the industry and local labor markets. We also offer a comprehensive medical and insurance coverage to meet the needs of our employees.

We are committed to creating a global workplace that supports diversity, equity and embraces inclusion. We welcome foreign nationals to join us through our Work Authorization Sponsorship.

We attract, develop, and retain exceptional people in an inclusive environment, where all employees can reach their greatest potential. We are dedicated to the ongoing development of our employees because we know that it is critical to our success as a global company.

We have established our Remote Work Policy for eligible roles to promote Work-Life Balance through a hybrid work set up where our team members can take advantage of working both from home and at the office.

Safety is paramount to us, and we are relentless in our pursuit to provide a Safe Working Environment across our global network and facilities.

Through our benefits, development opportunities, and an inclusive and safe work environment, we aim to create an organization our people are proud to represent.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

At Emerson, we are committed to fostering a culture where every employee is valued and respected for their unique experiences and perspectives. We believe a diverse and inclusive work environment contributes to the rich exchange of ideas and diversity of thoughts, that inspires innovation and brings the best solutions to our customers.

This philosophy is fundamental to living our company’s values and our responsibility to leave the world in a better place. Learn more about our Culture & Values and about Diversity, Equity & Inclusion at Emerson.

If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, please contact: idisability.administrator@emerson.com.

WHY EMERSON

Our Commitment to Our People

At Emerson, we are motivated by a spirit of collaboration that helps our diverse, multicultural teams across the world drive innovation that makes the world healthier, safer, smarter, and more sustainable. And we want you to join us in our bold aspiration.

We have built an engaged community of inquisitive, dedicated people who thrive knowing they are welcomed, trusted, celebrated, and empowered to solve the world’s most complex problems — for our customers, our communities, and the planet. You’ll contribute to this vital work while further developing your skills through our award-winning employee development programs. We are a proud corporate citizen in every city where we operate and are committed to our people, our communities, and the world at large. We take this responsibility seriously and strive to make a positive impact through every endeavor.

At Emerson, you’ll see firsthand that our people are at the center of everything we do. So, let’s go. Let’s think differently. Learn, collaborate, and grow. Seek opportunity. Push boundaries. Be empowered to make things better. Speed up to break through. Let’s go, together.

ABOUT EMERSON

Emerson is a global leader in automation technology and software. Through our deep domain expertise and legacy of flawless execution, Emerson helps customers in critical industries like life sciences, energy, power and renewables, chemical and advanced factory automation operate more sustainably while improving productivity, energy security and reliability.

With global operations and a comprehensive portfolio of software and technology, we are helping companies implement digital transformation to measurably improve their operations, conserve valuable resources and enhance their safety.

We offer equitable opportunities, celebrate diversity, and embrace challenges with confidence that, together, we can make an impact across a broad spectrum of countries and industries. Whether you’re an established professional looking for a career change, an undergraduate student exploring possibilities, or a recent graduate with an advanced degree, you’ll find your chance to make a difference with Emerson. Join our team – let’s go!

WHY EMERSON

Our Commitment to Our People

At Emerson, we are motivated by a spirit of collaboration that helps our diverse, multicultural teams across the world drive innovation that makes the world healthier, safer, smarter, and more sustainable. And we want you to join us in our bold aspiration.

We have built an engaged community of inquisitive, dedicated people who thrive knowing they are welcomed, trusted, celebrated, and empowered to solve the world’s most complex problems — for our customers, our communities, and the planet. You’ll contribute to this vital work while further developing your skills through our award-winning employee development programs. We are a proud corporate citizen in every city where we operate and are committed to our people, our communities, and the world at large. We take this responsibility seriously and strive to make a positive impact through every endeavor.

At Emerson, you’ll see firsthand that our people are at the center of everything we do. So, let’s go. Let’s think differently. Learn, collaborate, and grow. Seek opportunity. Push boundaries. Be empowered to make things better. Speed up to break through. Let’s go, together.

Accessibility Assistance or Accommodation

If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, please contact: idisability.administrator@emerson.com.

ABOUT EMERSON

Emerson is a global leader in automation technology and software. Through our deep domain expertise and legacy of flawless execution, Emerson helps customers in critical industries like life sciences, energy, power and renewables, chemical and advanced factory automation operate more sustainably while improving productivity, energy security and reliability.

With global operations and a comprehensive portfolio of software and technology, we are helping companies implement digital transformation to measurably improve their operations, conserve valuable resources and enhance their safety.

We offer equitable opportunities, celebrate diversity, and embrace challenges with confidence that, together, we can make an impact across a broad spectrum of countries and industries. Whether you’re an established professional looking for a career change, an undergraduate student exploring possibilities, or a recent graduate with an advanced degree, you’ll find your chance to make a difference with Emerson. Join our team – let’s go!

No calls or agencies please.
Experience Level
Senior Level

Responsibilities:
• Accurately process and verify invoice susing Zoho Books and QuickBooks, ensuring timely and precise entry.
• Manage accounts receivables, ensuring that all payments are tracked and collected in a timely manner.
• Prepare and submit GST and TDS reports, ensuring full compliance with regulations.
• Maintain detailed records of all incoming bills and outstanding payments.
• Generate and presentmonthly financial reports, focusing on accounts receivable performance and GST/TDS reconciliation.
• Provide clear and concise updates to management on key accounting issues and compliance matters.

Requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance,Business, or a related field.
• 3-6 years of experience in accounts receivables, invoice processing and compliance with GST/TDS regulations.
• Proficiency in MS Office, particularly Excel, and experience with accounting softwaresuch as Zoho Books and QuickBooks.
• Strong organizational skills,attention to detail,and the ability to handle multiple priorities.
• Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
• In-depth knowledge of GST, TDS reconciliation, and accounting compliance.
• Dedicated, professional, and focused on delivering high-quality service.

About the Role:

Zywave, Inc., a leading Insurtech SaaS provider is looking to add a Pune, India based Senior Accountant to its global corporate accounting team. The Senior Accountant is responsible for executing and improving general ledger accounting, the month-end close processes, account reconciliations, and financial analysis across multiple entities and currencies. This role ensures compliance with US GAAP, supports tax and audit activities, and drives process improvements, including automation initiates using AI-powered tools.

Key Responsibilities
• Manage and execute timely and accurate month-end and year-end close tasks, including journal entries, account reconciliations and variance analysis.
• Serve as the subject matter expert in technical accounting areas, including but not limited to Commissions, Leases, Fixed Assets, Prepaids, and Intercompany.
• Work closely with Finance, Operations, Legal, HR, and Sales to ensure accurate financial reporting.
• Identify and implement process improvements, including automation using AI-powered tools and bots.
• Ensure compliance with internal controls and statutory accounting guidance.

Essential Tasks:

Commissions Accounting: Partner with HR and Sales Operations to understand commission plans and ensure proper accounting treatment. Manage capitalized commission accounting, including identifying eligible costs, calculating amortization schedules, and maintaining supporting schedules and documentation. Record monthly commissions earned expense and reconcile related balance sheet accounts.

Lease Accounting: Manage end to end lease accounting in accordance with ASC 842, including classification, initial recognition, and ongoing measurement. Prepare monthly lease journal entries, reconciliations, and supporting documentation.

Fixed Assets: Oversee the fixed asset lifecycle, including capitalization, disposals, transfers, and depreciation. Maintain the fixed asset register and perform monthly reconciliations of fixed asset accounts and depreciation expense.

Prepaid Expenses: Manage the prepaid expense schedule and ensure timely amortization of balances. Review vendor invoices to determine proper prepaid treatment and coding. Prepare monthly journal entries and reconciliations for all prepaid accounts. Collaborate with department owners to validate timing and accuracy of prepaid allocations.

Other Tasks: Perform other assigned tasks which may include expense accruals, intercompany variance reconciliations, cash transactions, and other continuous improvement and strategic projects

Qualifications:
• Bachelor of Commerce (B. Com), specifically in Accounting & Finance (BAF) or USA bachelor’s degree in accounting.
• Indian Chartered Accountancy (CA) or USA Certified Public Accountant (CPA) preferred.
• Strong understanding of US GAAP.
• 3+ years of professional corporate accounting experience.
• Experience with month-end close including journal entries preparation, account reconciliations, and analysis.
• Excellent analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills.
• Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (e.g. pivot tables, VLOOKUP, macros).

Proficiency with the following systems is a plus:
• Sage Intacct
• Microsoft Office & Related Tools (e.g. Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Teams, Outlook, Power BI)
• Experience with AI and automation tools (e.g. Microsoft Copilot)

Why Zywave?

Zywave is a United States based company with operations in Pune India. Zywave empowers insurers and brokers to drive profitable growth and thrive in today’s escalating risk landscape. Only Zywave delivers a powerful Performance Multiplier, bringing together transformative, ecosystem-wide capabilities to amplify impact across data, processes, people, and customer experiences. More than 15,000 insurers, MGAs, agencies, and brokerages trust Zywave to sharpen risk assessment, strengthen client relationships, and enhance operations. Additional information can be found at www.zywave.com.

How to Apply

If you meet the above qualifications and are excited about the opportunity, please email your updated resume to Anindita.Acharjee@zywave.com with your availability and salary expectations. We will connect with you shortly regarding your fitment for the role.

data.job_title: Vice President, Financial Planning & Analysis Manager
data.job_description:
VICE PRESIDENT – ASSET SERVICING, FINANCIAL PLANNING & ANALYSIS

At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide.

Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what #LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary.

We’re seeking a future team member for the role of VICE PRESIDENT to join our Asset Servicing finance team. This role is located in Pune.

In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways:
• Serve as the core member of the Asset servicing reporting team
• Perform analyses—both historical and predictive—to support a range of finance processes. This includes period close activities, evaluating forecasted revenue and expenses against actual results, and delivering metrics and scorecard reports. Identify trends and present findings to finance leadership or other relevant business stakeholders.
• Lead the selection of methodologies and key assumptions in preparing short-term and long-range forecasts, ensuring these reflect shifts in business strategy and competitive intelligence impacting different business lines.
• Participate in developing annual operating plans. Gather and analyze financial information, conduct evaluations when necessary, and communicate findings and implications to senior leaders.
• Analyze the financial impact of new products and initiatives within Product Groups.
• Conduct advanced trend analysis on financial data and operational performance concerning highly complex topics.
• Contribute to the advancement of the finance team's strategic objectives
• Develop and implement process improvements leveraging automation and AI to enhance team efficiency and service standards.
• Attend governance meetings and forums as required.
• Build effective relationships across all Finance and relevant functional teams.
• Oversee the management unit hierarchy for the Product Group
• Take part in ad hoc projects as needed.

To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following:
• MBA or CA with 8-12 years of total work experience preferred; experience in financial planning and analysis preferred
• Team management experience preferred, but not required
• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills
• Good judgement, maturity and poise to enable interaction/influencing of senior management
• Thoughtful analytical skills; able to develop, clearly present and draw conclusions
• Self-starter: ability to prioritize, multi-task and meet deadlines
• Disciplined approach to managing processes and controls
• Energetic, self-motivated and effective under pressure
• Strong analytical and data manipulation skills; proficient in Microsoft Office – Excel, PowerPoint
• Preferred - Practical knowledge of building Power BI dashboards and/or AI solutions

At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at:

BNY Newsroom

BNY LinkedIn

Here’s a few of our recent awards:
• America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025
• World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025
• “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025

Our Benefits and Rewards:

BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay-for-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter.

BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer - Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

data.job_title: Walk-In Drive for Capital Market on 14th March-Pune, Hinjewadi Phase 3

data.job_description:
At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges—delivering critical insights and innovative solutions that enable our customers to achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments. With our global reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to shape your future and make a lasting impact. Are you ready to make a difference?

Job Description:

Ecolab is seeking an FP&A Director responsible for leading financial planning and analysis activities across the organization. This role serves as a key point of contact for Division Finance Managers and enables them to drive best‑in‑class financial performance and business insights. The position requires strong partnerships across multiple stakeholders and offers significant exposure and growth opportunities. As FP&A Director, you will work closely with divisional finance teams to drive results and strengthen financial rigor.

What’s in it for You?
• Join a fast-growing organization offering a competitive salary and benefits.
• Influence and shape your career with a company committed to continuous growth.
• Work in an environment that values diverse people, perspectives, and ideas.
• Feel proud to contribute to a company that ensures clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments.

What You Will Do
• Lead a team of division FP&A managers in GBS+ and ensure timely completion of divisional analytics packs with robust insights explaining business performance drivers.
• Ensure delivery of services in alignment with established service-level agreements (SLAs) with divisions.
• Partner with Process Leads to drive divisional FP&A transformation initiatives.
• Collaborate closely with division finance leaders to help FP&A managers understand internal and external business dynamics and their impact on financial outcomes.
• Lead and coordinate the development of annual plans, ensuring strong collaboration among all key stakeholders.
• Oversee monthly close and forecast processes—including providing direction, reviewing results, supporting system loads, and preparing content for senior leadership business reviews.
• Ensure teams deliver accurate, sustainable content for monthly business reviews (MBRs) and provide ad hoc financial analysis for decision‑making.
• Collaborate with broader finance and business teams to support critical reporting needs and enhance reporting capabilities and tools.

Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or Economics
• CA or equivalent professional qualification preferred
• Minimum 18 years of finance experience, with strong exposure to FP&A
• Experience in an FMCG environment strongly preferred
• Proficiency in BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI
• Strong understanding of financial and commercial reporting fundamentals
• Exceptional analytical, problem‑solving, and decision‑making skills
• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast‑paced environment

Our Commitment to a Culture of Inclusion & Belonging
Ecolab is committed to fair and equal treatment of associates and applicants and furthering the principles of Equal Opportunity to Employment. We will recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement based on individual qualifications and job performance in all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement. Ecolab will not discriminate against any associate or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin,citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, or disability.

data.job_title: Lead Analyst - FP&A and Power BI
data.job_description:
Responsibilities

Lead Analyst - Accounting

Exp - 6 - 10 years

Shift Timings - 1.30 PM - 10.30 PM
• Build, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards to support forecasting, performance tracking, and management reporting for SCBUs.
• Design intuitive, leadership‑friendly visualizations that highlight KPIs, financial trends, and business drivers.
• Automate manual reports and recurring processes using Power BI, Power Query, and other analytical tools to improve efficiency and reduce turnaround time.
• Collect, clean, aggregate, and validate data from multiple SCBUs and enterprise systems to ensure accuracy and reliability of reporting.
• Establish and maintain data links between different sources, building data models, and maintaining reporting datasets to support end‑to‑end dashboard operations. (Power BI datasets, Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Advanced Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) to create a seamless reporting ecosystem.
• Support forecasting, actuals reporting, and value analysis by preparing data inputs and performing first‑level variance reviews.
• Provide initial analytical support for performance metrics, and operational KPIs, highlighting anomalies and key insights.
• Assist in documenting processes, data flows, and dashboard refresh cycles.
• Highlight trends, outliers, and data inconsistencies.
• Support the SCBU FP&A team in process redesign, reporting improvements, and system enhancements to align with evolving business requirements.
• Work collaboratively with SCBU FP&A teams and cross‑BU finance partners to share best practices and ensure alignment of data and reporting standards.
• Gain a strong understanding of financial data structures, systems, and reporting tools used within the organization.
• Manage assigned deliverables independently, including prioritizing multiple tasks, meeting deadlines, and maintaining high‑quality outputs.
• Prepare clear, visually effective presentations and insights for leadership consumption.

Qualifications

Education & Experience
• Master’s degree / Professional Degree / Relevant Industry Certification
• Preferably Chartered Accountant or MBA Finance
• 6-10 years’ of relevant experience in handling complex datasets, analytics, reporting, automation & PowerBI skills. (preferably in BFSI Industry, either in GCC / service provider environment)

Technical Skills
• Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI—data modeling, DAX, dashboards, and report publishing.
• Proficiency in Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query), PowerPoint.
• Comfort working with large datasets and connecting to varied data sources.
• Basic understanding of financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet).
• Experience with data cleansing, validation, and transformation.
• Exposure to SQL or other database/querying tools

Analytical & Soft Skills
• Strong problem‑solving ability and high attention to detail.
• Good communication skills with ability to translate data into insights.
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks, and prioritize effectively.
• Eagerness to learn and develop deeper financial acumen.

Our team members are at the heart of everything we do. At Cencora, we are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures, and every person here is essential to us being able to deliver on that purpose. If you want to make a difference at the center of health, come join our innovative company and help us improve the lives of people and animals everywhere. Apply today!

Job Details
• Financial Planning & Analysis: Input and manage financial data related to Plan, Estimate, and Long Range Plan (LRP) into our Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) system.
• Monthly Reporting: assist in preparation of monthly reporting packages and other business presentations as required; prepare ad hoc reports as needed.
• Analysis & Modeling: Evaluate, estimate, and analyze financial and operational impacts of new business prospects.
• Variance Reporting: Identify and report on variances/changes by reconciling data within various financial systems and data warehouse applications.
• Provide in-depth analysis of deviations.
• Expense Management: Oversee operating expenses for departments including operations, distribution, and commercial organizations that drive key distribution channels.
• Analytical Reporting: Generate year-over-year, quarter-over-quarter, and month-to-date analysis comparing actual results against Plan/Estimate/Prior year metrics for key expense accounts and business drivers.
• Collaboration With Finance Team: Partner with the Finance team to ensure timely updates to estimates and provide monthly financial reporting to senior management.

.

Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting or related field; CPA or CFA certification is a plus.
• Minimum of 3 years’ experience in financial planning & analysis within a large corporation or global business service environment.
• Proficiency in Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) systems and Workday.
• Strong analytical skills with advanced proficiency in Excel; experience with data visualization tools is advantageous.
• Excellent communication skills with demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across different teams.
